name: Maui Teachers
description_short: Maui Teachers Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving educators in Hawaii with deposit accounts, lending, and ATM/debit card services since 1936.
description_long:
Maui Teachers Federal Credit Union was established in 1936, making it nearly 90 years old. As a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Wailuku, Hawaii, it operates with a specific mission to serve educational professionals in the Maui region. The institution has built a modest but stable presence on the islands with approximately $35 million in total assets and 1,290 members.

The credit union offers core consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and other lending products. Based on their asset allocation, they maintain a loan portfolio of approximately $11.7 million across real estate ($2.99M), unsecured loans ($563K), and other loan categories ($8.19M). They maintain one physical branch location at their main office in Wailuku and employ 4 full and part-time staff members led by CEO Kelly K Tobita.

What distinguishes Maui Teachers from other Hawaii-based credit unions is their narrow membership focus on educators, which provides a tight-knit community structure typical of occupational credit unions. Their long operational history in the Maui market demonstrates sustained local presence and member loyalty. They maintain federal insurance through NCUSIF and operate independently without corporate credit union status, meaning they manage their own capital and risk directly.

However, prospective members should recognize significant constraints: with only $35M in assets and 1,290 members, this is a very small financial institution with limited technological infrastructure implied by single-branch operation. Their recent financial statements show modest net income of $149,938 and negative other reserves of -$1.02M, suggesting tight operational margins. The membership restriction to educational professionals means many consumers cannot join. For those eligible, this credit union represents a traditional, community-focused alternative to larger institutions, but with correspondingly limited services, technology, and product depth.
services: Money market accounts (other shares and deposits)
Real estate loans (mortgages and home loans)
Financial counseling implied by member advisory board structure
pros: 90-year operational history demonstrates institutional stability and deep community roots in Maui
Member-owned cooperative structure ensures governance prioritizes member interests rather than shareholder profits
Federally insured through NCUSIF for deposit protection up to standard limits
Offers real estate lending in addition to consumer loans, enabling mortgage and home equity products
Low employee-to-member ratio (4 staff for 1,290 members) suggests personalized service potential
CEO and board leadership listed with named officers indicating transparent governance
ATM/debit card program access for digital banking convenience
cons: Very small institution ($35M assets) limits product offerings, technology platforms, and service innovation compared to larger credit unions
Single branch location restricts physical access for members outside immediate Wailuku area
Membership restricted to educational professionals—most consumers cannot qualify to join
Negative reserve balances (-$1.02M) and modest profitability suggest limited financial cushion during economic stress
No mention of online banking, mobile apps, or modern digital services on available information
best_for: Educators and school administrators working in Maui County seeking community-focused banking
Local Maui residents with educational employment background preferring credit union membership over commercial banks
Members wanting personalized service and local decision-making from a trusted 90-year-old institution
Borrowers seeking real estate loans with a lender familiar with Maui's local market conditions
diagnosis: Maui Teachers Federal Credit Union is exclusively for educators in the Maui region seeking traditional, relationship-based banking from a stable 90-year-old institution. The primary caveat is extreme size limitation—with $35M in assets and single-branch operation, this credit union cannot compete with larger institutions on technology, product breadth, or service hours, making it viable only for geographically proximate, eligible members who prioritize personal service over convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
